title: दत्तात्रेय स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दत्तात्रेय स्तोत्रम् ॥

जटाधरं पाण्डुराङ्गं शूलहस्तं कृपानिधिम्।
सर्वरोगहरं देवं दत्तात्रेयमहं भजे॥१॥

अस्य श्रीदत्तात्रेयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य भगवान् नारदऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीदत्तपरमात्मा देवता।
श्रीदत्तप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

जगदुत्पत्तिकर्त्रे च स्थितिसंहारहेतवे।
भवपाशविमुक्ताय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥१॥

जराजन्मविनाशाय देहशुद्धिकराय च।
दिगम्बरदयामूर्ते दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥२॥

कर्पूरकान्तिदेहाय ब्रह्ममूर्तिधराय च।
वेदशास्त्रपरिज्ञाय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥३॥

ह्रस्वदीर्घकृशस्थूलनामगोत्रविवर्जित।
पञ्चभूतैकदीपाय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥४॥

यज्ञभोक्ते च यज्ञाय यज्ञरूपधराय च।
यज्ञप्रियाय सिद्धाय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥५॥

आदौ ब्रह्मा मध्ये विष्णुरन्ते देवः सदाशिवः।
मूर्तित्रयस्वरूपाय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥६॥

भोगालयाय भोगाय योगयोग्याय धारिणे।
जितेन्द्रियजितज्ञाय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥७॥

दिगम्बराय दिव्याय दिव्यरूपधराय च।
सदोदितपरब्रह्म दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥८॥

जम्बूद्वीपमहाक्षेत्रमातापुरनिवासिने।
जयमानसतां देव दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥९॥

भिक्षाटनं गृहे ग्रामे पात्रं हैमवतं करे।
नानास्वादमयी भिक्षा दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥१०॥

त्रिगुणात्मकसंयुक्तं पालनाय प्रजापते।
अनसूयात्रिपुत्राय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥११॥

दत्तविद्याढ्यलक्ष्मीशं दत्तस्वात्मस्वरूपिणे।
गुणनिर्गुणरूपाय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥१२॥

शरणागतदीनार्तपरित्राणपरायण।
सर्वार्तिहरदेवेश दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥१३॥

शरणं त्वत्पदाम्भोजं दत्तात्रेय जगत्प्रभो।
त्वामृते नहि मे त्राता दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥१४॥

सर्वानर्थहरं देवं सर्वमङ्गलमङ्गलम्।
सर्वक्लेशहरं वन्दे दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥१५॥

ब्रह्मज्ञानमयी मुद्रा वस्त्रे चाकाशभूतले।
प्रज्ञानघनबोधाय दत्तात्रेय नमोऽस्तु ते॥१६॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
योऽनुदिनमिदं स्तोत्रं दत्तात्रेयस्य यो पठेत्।
दत्तात्रेयप्रसादेन हरते सकलापदः॥

॥ इति श्री नारदपुराणोक्तं दत्तात्रेय स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Dattātreya Stotram ||

Jaṭādharaṁ pāṇḍurāṅgaṁ śūlahastaṁ kṛpānidhim.
Sarvarogaharaṁ devaṁ Dattātreyamahaṁ bhaje.||1||

Asya Shrīdattātreyastotra mantrasya Bhagavān Nārada ṛṣiḥ.
Anuṣṭup chhandaḥ. Shrī Dattaparamātmā devatā.
Shrīdattaprītyarthe jape viniyogaḥ.

Jagadutpattikarttre cha sthitisaṁhārahetave.
Bhavapāśavimuktāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||1||

Jarājanmavināśāya dehaśuddhikarāya cha.
Digambaradayāmūrte Dattātreya namo'stu te.||2||

Karpūrakāntidehāya Brahmamūrtidharāya cha.
Vedaśāstraparidjñāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||3||

Hrasvadīrghakṛśasthūlanāmagotravivarjita.
Pañchabhūtaikadīpāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||4||

Yajñabhokte cha yajñāya yajñarūpadharāya cha.
Yajñapriyāya siddhāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||5||

Ādau Brahmā madhye Viṣṇurunte devaḥ Sadāśivaḥ.
Mūrtitrayasvarūpāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||6||

Bhogālayāya bhogāya yogayogyāya dhāriṇe.
Jitendriyajitajñāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||7||

Digambarāya divyāya divyarūpadharāya cha.
Sadoditaparabrahma Dattātreya namo'stu te.||8||

Jambūdvīpamahākṣetramātāpuranivāsine.
Jayamānasatāṁ deva Dattātreya namo'stu te.||9||

Bhikṣāṭanaṁ gṛhe grāme pātraṁ haimavataṁ kare.
Nānāsvādamayī bhikṣā Dattātreya namo'stu te.||10||

Triguṇātmakasaṁyuktaṁ pālanāya Prajāpate.
Anasūyātriputrāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||11||

Dattavidyāḍhyalakṣmīśaṁ dattasvātmasvarūpiṇe.
Guṇanirguṇarūpāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||12||

Śaraṇāgatadīnārtaparitrāṇaparāyaṇa.
Sarvārtihara deveśa Dattātreya namo'stu te.||13||

Śaraṇaṁ tvatpadāmbhojaṁ Dattātreya jagatprabho.
Tvāmṛte nahi me trātā Dattātreya namo'stu te.||14||

Sarvānarthaharaṁ devaṁ sarvamaṅgalamaṅgalam.
Sarvakleśaharaṁ vande Dattātreya namo'stu te.||15||

Brahmajñānamayī mudrā vastre chākāśabhūtale.
Prajñānaghanaboddhāya Dattātreya namo'stu te.||16||

|| Phalaśruti ||
Yo'nudinamidaṁ stotraṁ Dattātreyasya yo paṭhet.
Dattātreyaprasādena harate sakalāpadaḥ.

|| Iti Shrī Nāradapurāṇoktaṁ Dattātreya Stotraṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: जटाधारी, गौर अंग वाले, शूल हाथ में धारण करने वाले, कृपा के भण्डार, सभी रोगों को हरने वाले दत्तात्रेय भगवान का मैं भजन करता हूँ। जगत की उत्पत्ति, स्थिति और संहार के कारण, भव-पाश से मुक्त करने वाले दत्तात्रेय को नमस्कार। जरा और जन्म का विनाश करने वाले, देह को शुद्ध करने वाले, दिगम्बर दया की मूर्ति दत्तात्रेय को नमस्कार। कर्पूर जैसी कान्ति वाले शरीर, ब्रह्म की मूर्ति धारण करने वाले, वेद-शास्त्रों के ज्ञाता दत्तात्रेय को नमस्कार।
benefits: Classical tradition invokes this for removal of calamities and dangers from life
Traditionally recited for removal of the cycle of birth, death, and old age
Purifies the body, mind, and soul
Invokes knowledge of vedas and scriptures
Frees from bondage of worldly attachments
Invokes the blessing of the combined blessings of brahma, vishnu, and shiva
description: Dattatreya Stotram (दत्तात्रेय स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Dattatreya. This revered text originates from Narada Purana. Regular recitation is believed to remove all calamities and dangers from life, purify the body, mind, and soul, and free from bondage of worldly attachments. This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Guru Purnima to invoke divine grace. At 16 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Dattatreya Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Dattatreya Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn from Narada Purana for spiritual growth and blessings of the Trimurti.
relatedSearches: Dattatreya Stotram lyrics Sanskrit Devanagari PDF
Anasuya Atri parents of Dattatreya story
Trimurti Brahma Vishnu Shiva Dattatreya iconography

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dattatreya Stotram and which deity does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dattatreya Stotram is a 16-verse Sanskrit hymn from the Narada Purana, composed by sage Narada in the anushtup metre, praising Lord Dattatreya — the unique Trimurti incarnation in whom Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva are simultaneously present. The opening verse Jatadharam Pandurangam describes him as bearing matted locks (jata), pale-bodied (pandurangam), holding the trident (shulahastam), an ocean of compassion (kripanidhim), and the destroyer of all diseases (sarva-roga-haram). The hymn praises him as the cause of cosmic creation-preservation-dissolution, the dispeller of birth-death-old age, the brahma-rupa, the yajna-bhokta, and the eternal mendicant carrying the bhiksha-patra. (Sources: Narada Purana; Stotra Nidhi Dattatreya hymns; Hindupedia Dattatreya.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Dattatreya and how was he born?
seoFaqs.answer: Dattatreya is the unique combined incarnation of Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva, born as the son of sage Atri (a Saptarishi) and Anasuya (the embodiment of unwavering chastity). According to the Bhagavata Purana and Markandeya Purana, the three gods came to test Anasuya's pativrata-dharma; she sprinkled them with water and converted them into infants. Pleased by her devotion, the three combined and were born as a single divine child with three heads and six arms — Datta (the given) + Atreya (son of Atri). The eleventh verse of this stotra explicitly invokes him as Anasuya-tri-putra (son of Anasuya and Atri, three-formed). He is the Adi Guru of the Avadhuta tradition. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 4.1; Markandeya Purana; Sri Gurucharitra.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the major incarnations of Dattatreya in tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Dattatreya tradition recognises a continuous lineage of avataras: the original Dattatreya born to Atri-Anasuya in Treta Yuga; Sripada Srivallabha (1320-1350 CE) born at Pithapuram, the first historical Datta avatara; Narasimha Saraswati (1378-1458 CE) born at Karanja, who lived at Ganagapur and authored the Sri Gurucharitra-narrative; Janardana Swami and his disciple Eknath Maharaj of Maharashtra; Manik Prabhu (1817-1865) of Humnabad; Akkalkot Swami Samarth (d. 1878) regarded as the third complete Datta avatara; Sai Baba of Shirdi (per some traditions); and Vasudevananda Saraswati (Tembe Swami, 1854-1914). The audumbar (cluster fig) tree is sacred to all Datta avataras. (Sources: Sri Gurucharitra; Akkalkot Maharaj Trust; Hindupedia Datta avataras.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Dattatreya Stotram be recited for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) is the supreme weekday for Datta-upasana. The grandest occasion is Datta Jayanti — Margashirsha Purnima (December full moon) — when grand utsavas are held at Ganagapur, Pithapuram, Audumbar, Akkalkot, Mahur, Kuravpur and Nrisimhawadi, the seven sacred Datta-kshetras. Other powerful days include every Krittika nakshatra day (Datta's birth nakshatra), Guru Purnima (Ashadha Purnima), the entire Margashirsha month, all eclipses, and brahma-muhurta daily. Devotees observing Datta-Saptaha (seven-day vrata before Datta Jayanti) recite the stotra 1, 3, 7 or 108 times daily for forty days as Datta-anushthana. (Sources: Drikpanchang Datta Jayanti; Sri Gurucharitra; Akkalkot Trust.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Dattatreya Stotram grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares that whoever recites this hymn daily has all calamities removed by Dattatreya's grace (harate sakalapadah). Verses within the stotra promise removal of disease (sarva-roga-haram), liberation from bhava-pasha (worldly bondage), destruction of old age and rebirth (jara-janma-vinashaya), purification of the body (deha-shuddhi), brahma-jnana (verse 16: prajnana-ghana-bodha), the combined blessings of Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva, freedom from all anartha (calamities), and the protection of one who has surrendered (sharanagata-paritrana). Datta-upasakas testify to direct guru-darshan, often in dream as Datta or as one of his historical avataras. (Sources: Narada Purana phalashruti; Sri Gurucharitra; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the iconography of Dattatreya described in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The first verse paints Dattatreya as Jatadharam (matted-haired ascetic), Pandurangam (pale-bodied like sandalwood), Shulahastam (trident-bearing), Kripanidhim (ocean of mercy). Other verses add Karpura-kanti-deha (camphor-bright body), Brahma-murti-dhara, Digambara (sky-clad), Pancha-bhuta-eka-dipa (one lamp of the five elements), and the iconic Bhikshatanam (mendicant) carrying a haimavata-patra (golden bowl). Popular Datta iconography depicts him with three heads (Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva), six arms (carrying trishula, damaru, mala, kamandalu, shankha, chakra), accompanied by four dogs (the four Vedas) and a cow (Kamadhenu, mother earth), with the audumbar tree behind. (Sources: Narada Purana; Sri Gurucharitra; Hindupedia Datta iconography.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Dattatreya Stotram differ from Datta Bavanni and Sri Gurucharitra?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are central to Datta-bhakti but vary in language and scope. The Dattatreya Stotram (16 Sanskrit verses, Narada Purana) is the classical iconographic praise used in pan-Indian Datta-puja — short enough for daily recitation. The Datta Bavanni is a popular Gujarati 52-verse devotional composed by Rang Avadhut (Pandurang Vitthal Valame, 1898-1968) — extremely beloved in Gujarat and Maharashtra for daily recitation with reported chamatkara (miraculous) effects. The Sri Gurucharitra is a much longer 51-chapter Marathi scripture by Sri Saraswati Gangadhar (16th century) narrating the lives of Sripada Srivallabha and Narasimha Saraswati — read as parayana (continuous reading) over 7 days, especially during Datta-saptaha. (Sources: Sri Gurucharitra editions; Datta Bavanni publications; Akkalkot Maharaj Trust.)
